text: Interrupted Lesson
circa 10 ABY
Jessalyn Valios
Uwannabuyim
Jessalyn Valios, Orson Tighe, Han Solo, Drew Carvallo
Jedi, New Republic
While Jessa teaches Orson about telekinesis, they receive a few surprise visitors.

Main Corridor - Uwannabuyim%rThis is a good sized, round room. To the port is a set of benches in the%rwalls, a little nook, surrounding a holochess table. Beside that,%rrunning along the wall to where the short corridor to the fore section%ris, is a series of terminals and computers, whose primary function is%rextra-cockpit monitoring; cargo, sensors, weapons control, et cetera.%rThe low bubble of a ceiling is dark, graphite colored, metallic. The%rfloor is much the same, though composed of grates instead of sheeting.%rTo the starboard, there is a door leading into a stateroom for%rpassengers.

Drew is a tall, leggy blonde. You know the type; legs up to her chin,%rarms down to her ankles, lots and lots of crazy blonde hair. Her bedroom%reyes are of a grayish, peridot green, her skin is a peachy tan, and her%rnose is heavily freckled. The hair is of a honey color, in artfully%rdisheveled waves down to her shoulders. She's past her teens, and%rprobably most of her twenties too, but it's hard to tell with her. She%rhas the kind of body an athlete would have, good shoulders, a narrow%rwaist, and coltish limbs. Her cheeks have a constant blush to them, much%rlike some who live in cold weather; her nose seems to have been broken%rsometime, it is a tad long and slightly hooked.

She wears a standard spacer's outfit. Comfortable brown pants reinforced%rat the knee with darker leather, tucked into soft ankle boots and a tan,%rstretchy shirt. If weather and situation call for it, she carries a%rblaster in a shoulder holster under a dark brown leather jacket.

Han Solo%rThis tall, rangy man moves with the loose and confident motions of a%rfighter, someone accustomed to getting into tight situations... and%rgetting quickly right back out of them. His brown hair is cut%rpragmatically short, but is thick enough to hold a hint of a wave,%rframing a set of ruggedly handsome features that have finally lost the%rlast traces of youthfulness and are solidly into weathered maturity. A%rlong scar crooks across his chin, adding another touch of ruggedness to%rhis face. Sharp-gazed hazel eyes, prone to shift tint depending on his%rclothing, miss very little that crosses their line of sight, and he%rtypically speaks in a resonant, gravelly baritone.

He is currently clad in a simple black vest over a white shirt, tucked%rinto military-blue pants, notable by the single red stripe that runs%rdown the side of each leg, and scuffed black boots. All of his clothing%rappears to have seen better days, although it's perfectly serviceable.%rAround his waist is slung a blaster belt, tilted down slightly at an%rangle towards the holster riding on his right thigh.

Orson%rToo short, not handsome, and a little too old. What's lacking in looks%rhas to be made up for with something strong on the inside: determination%rand persistence, a certain grit evident in the look sent by his slate%rgray eyes. Lines around this human male's mouth and eyes tell of hard%rdays and decisions in his past, each one a new crease in an otherwise%ryoung man's face. He is smaller framed, though quite stout with a barrel%rchest and strong shoulders. Still, he's not overly muscled, simply in%rgood physical shape. Dark hair is kept in a simple style but is more%roften than not in a disheveled state. A few lonely gray hairs touch his%rtemples. He might be around forty standard years old. He has a larger%rnose, on a round-shaped, bold face that is quick with a grin but usually%rcaught up in a shade of thoughtful. About a week's worth of trimmed%rbeard covers his face, peppered with dark gray; the facial hair lends%rOrson's face additional depth and distinction.%rHe is wearing neutral gray trousers, made of a thick fabric, only%rremarkable in that they represent hylomorphic "pants". A simple but%rheavy jacket, made of similar but darker cloth, hangs on his shoulders.%rWhere it parts in front, a form-fitting white shirt with straight%rstripes shows itself. Dark boots round out the wardrobe. Despite its%rsimplicity and economy, every garment is clean and well-kept. Even if%runassuming, details are important to this man.

Jessalyn%rThe composure of this young human woman is probably the most striking%rthing about her. Though otherwise unassuming, her expression is one of%rsurprising coherence and calm, belied only by the slightly mischievous%rgleam in her leaf green eyes. Shining dark red hair falls in unruly%rsilken waves down to the middle of her back, framing her wide cheekbones%rand smooth, pale skin not as fragile as most redheads'. She is%rrelatively tall for a human woman, with long-boned limbs and a natural%rgrace amplified by her skills.%rShe is wearing a dark green, long-sleeved shirt beneath a velvet black%rtunic that is belted at her narrow waist. The full sleeves are cinched%rabove her pale, slender wrists. A pair of tight, dark green pants are%rtucked into knee-high black leather boots, both complementing the best%rpair of legs in ten parsecs.
There was definitely something wrong with the polishing motor.

Just as Orson was completing his work on the second lightsaber crystal,%rthe thing had given a wheeze and screeched as metal ground on metal,%rsome essential component warped beyond recognition. After inspection,%rJessalyn decided that she could probably repair it using her tools back%ron board the ship. And so, with her thoughts turning to another plan for%rthe day, she takes Orson with her back to the Uwannabuyim. For a brief%rmoment, Jessa remembers the days when her most constant companion was%rChewbacca the Wookiee -- when Han Solo had been imprisoned by the CSA,%rof course -- thinking how handy it would be to have him around to lug%rall this lightsaber-constructing equipment for them. The thought makes%rher laugh, realizing how silly a notion that is when all she has to do%ris exert a little Force energy to help lighten Orson's load on her own.

As they climb up the ramp, she hangs her jacket on a hook by the door%rand crosses into the main hold. "I thought we might try something else%rtoday," she says, taking on a teacherly tone for the first time in a%rwhile, even as she starts poking around for her toolbox.

If it wasn't one thing it was another with this quest for a lightsaber.%rStill, the mechanic had grown more patient with his efforts to construct%rthe device, and had found that his work had taken on an almost reluctant%rquality. His inner drive and long experience at managing a task kept the%rwhole thing from derailing. That, and the fact that there seemed to be%rsomething larger at work in the lightsaber. She had been right about%rthat: it was more than simply bringing a lot of parts together. It was%rmore like art, really. Once he had realized that, the process was%runfolded. It was clear what he had to do.
"Okay," Orson agrees simply, keen ear detecting the intent in her words.%rHe drops his cargo on a temporary workspace set up near the%rUwannabuyim's large datastation.

Pulling something resembling a tiny blowtorch and a pair of goggles out%rof the large metal toolkit, Jessalyn turns and goes back to Orson's%rside, placing the items on the datastation. "You've been very patient,"%rshe says, comforting him a little about the continued delays in his%rwork. "But it won't be long now, I have a feeling."

She glances around, hands on her hips, lips pursed. Then, decisively,%rshe turns and easily lifts the crate, placing it in the center of the%rfloor, and then resuming a seated, cross-legged position a few meters%raway. She gestures to the floor, suggesting he do the same, and taps her%rfingers on her knees. "Okay. This is something we haven't really tried%ryet," Jessa says cheerfully. "Unless you've tried it on your own? Think%ryou can lift this?"

Orson listens, removing his own jacket as she speaks, suddenly aware%rthat this task will include some exertion. "Thanks for all your help,"%rhe mentions, quietly. Polite fellow, despite his level of familiarity%rwith her. Balling up his jacket on the chair behind him, Orson takes a%rbreath, chest filling out the straight-striped shirt which covers him.

He eases down and mimics her posture well enough, body folding almost%rnaturally into the postition. "With the Force you mean," the learner%rsays, not really asking and eyeing the heavy box. "How, exactly?"%rHowever it happens, Orson knows the process to go through before he does%rany exercise, and his eyes blink a few times as he relaxes his mind.%rHe's suddenly more conscious and aware, finding it easier to take that%rparticular first step every time he does it.

As she always does, Jessalyn makes her own interior preparations,%rfeeling the Force begin to ripple as Orson concentrates, coursing along%rthe intricately woven bond between them. It now seems to Jessa that it%rcould have been there forever, and she really can't quite imagine living%rwithout its tender support.

Chiding herself for daydreaming again, she focuses on the Force,%rglancing inwardly toward Orson across from her. "Feel the Force surround%ryou. It connects you to everything. It exists everywhere, and all you%rhave to do is give it the command that you want... imagine that energy%rlifting the crate as if it were a pebble you could pick up...."

Just a month or two ago, Orson would have been wearing an extremely%rskeptical look on his face when he heard this. Now, however, he simply%rfurrows his brow, gives Jessalyn a reluctant nod, and steadies himself.%rHis eyes close. She was easy enough to feel through the Force, the%rcouple like a pair of binary objects that rotated around each other. The%rship was remarkably easy to feel, his own heart having been poured into%rthe ship's cold metal frame often enough. Even the polishing wheel. He%rcould make out a whisper of it from its spot within the crate. There was%ra bond there too.

He seems in no hurry, simply finding the corners of the box, making a%rmental note of where things are, and drifting to another corner. His%rattention is methodical in that way, and in this scenario, it works%ragainst him as he can't feel the whole as well as the parts. Suddenly,%rhe lifts his chin, eyes still closed. A hand has wandered out from his%rbody, into the air between them, Orson's fingers open like he's holding%ra miniature crate. The Force ripples, even flexes around the box, but it%rdoesn't move. His ability to sense is fine enough to know that he hasn't%rdone it, so he takes another breath, and presses on.

Jessalyn chews on her lower lip as she observes with all her senses, her%rbreathing light and shallow in her own concentration. This was a harder%rlesson than it seemed for the novice Jedi student, and she remembers%rmany a headache she gave herself in trying to lift even the smallest of%robjects at first. But she believes that Luke is right -- Orson may very%rwell be more powerful than she is. And it seemed to her that the very%rlink between them seemed to only strengthen each of them. Her strength%rwas shared with him in a way that was revealing the Force in profound%rnew ways for even herself. And for Orson, it meant a deeper glimpse of%rwhat his powers really meant, even at this early stage.
She finds herself holding her breath, sensing that he hasn't given up%rafter his first failed effort. "You're trying too hard, I think," she%radvises cautiously.

Lowering his hand, Orson's concentration is broken, hold on the crate%revaporating like water disappearing with a flash. His eyes creep open,%rand he nods at the woman seated across from him. Another few minutes%rpass, and he's back in a similar spot. He's in a cold sweat.

Orson can't get his mind off of the pile of pebbles in his pocket. Mira%rhad given them to him when he shared his news about training with the%rstrange girl. She said he'd need them, for training. This seemed an%rappropriate time to wonder about them, since that was their intended%rpurpose. Pebbles seemed more fair than crates, Orson considered. With%rthe smallest of squeaks and the most gentle of touches, the crate%rshifts, and then is still. This time, Orson maintains his concentration%rand lifts his hand once more, feeling it now.

Jessalyn and Orson are both seated on the deck in the center of the main%rhold, with a large and apparently heavy crate resting on the floor%rbetween them. Disappointment shows on her face for a moment when Orson's%rinitial efforts produce no results. Sending a little reassuring smile%rthrough the Force as well as on her face, she can't help but chuckle.%r"It shouldn't matter the size," she says chidingly. "The crate is%rnothing but a pebble in the Force. You can lift a pebble, a crate, a%rship, if you let the Force guide you."

Orson's hand remains stretched out, into the air, his fingers twitching%rlightly like they are feeling the individual notes of some music that's%rdrifting through the Uwannabuyim. A stripe of sweat has dampened a road%rfrom Orson's hairline to his chin, moisture clinging to his face and%rrough beard. He seems absolutely frozen in this posture, afraid to move%rlest it break his careful concentration. Slowly, like a wire from the%rceiling is pulling it up, one side of the crate lifts. Then another,%runtil only a point is resting on the ground. Slowly, that comes up as%rwell, the heavy crate rotating softly in the air.

"Guys, guys!" Drew calls out as she bursts through the entrance hatch.%rShe sounds cheerful, loud, happy, "You wouldn't BELIEVE what I just%rbought..." There is a bag slung on her back, and she's wearing tinted%rglasses, looking all bright and blonde and sunburned. She should be on%rthe cover of a shopping holomagazine. There's a crate floating in the%rair. She blinks at it, greenish eyes wide.

Resting on his haunches, Orson is leaning forward, eyes closed, like the%rcrate is pulling him forward, controlling the mechanic instead of the%rother way around. Drew's first word snaps his hold on the object,%rhowever, and, just like any other crate dropped in mid-air, the thing%rtumbles to the ground. A wave of mechanical parts inside it slosh around%rin the container, its sides buckling when it falls the short distance.%rThe mechanic's eyes snap open, already trained on Jessalyn. They have%rregret in them, but are more full of apology than anything else. "I did%rit," he whispers, even as Drew stands there. The man swivels his head,%rsmoothly, heart pounding in his chest and face covered in sweat. "What%rwas it?" he asks, looking the tall woman over, hardly skipping a beat. A%rgrin forms easily on his face.

Blinking in shock, Jessalyn's concentration also breaks when Drew bursts%rinto the hold, and she is unable to keep the crate from crashing into a%rbattered heap on the deck. Groaning with her own stupidity, she smiles a%rlittle dumbly at Drew, and then moves to inspect the spilled parts,%rhoping nothing else was damaged. "Hi, Drew," she greets, picking up a%rhydrospanner and testing the gauge. "Um. What's up?"
You sent through the Force to Orson...
Jessalyn's regret is nearly palpable... she isn't comfortable with%rpressing the issue with Drew or any of the Karrde associates. "Orson,%rI'm sorry, I didn't know...."

Drew is starting to think she should knock. Or maybe just...lock herself%rin. Sigh. It's not all that hard to tell that she interrupted something%rimportant. The grin on her face drops, like an anvil, from rapture to%rsurprise to blinking, perplexed sheepishness. Goshdarnit, she assumes%rthat Jessalyn was the one making the crate float, and who knows what she%rthinks Orson is doing, sweating and straining like that, but she's sorry%rshe came in. She doesn't really want to bother anyone. Her voice drops,%r"An, um, a portable holochess set. The resolution is fuzzy, but I'm sure%rone of you techpeople can fix it." Blinkblink.

%tWell. What a nice day, on Caspar. For what Caspar was. And the sun%rhad gone down about an hour ago, so, that wasn't why the dour Han Solo%rhad walked out of his ship, with two bottles of Corellian Brandy, and%rset straight for the Uwannabuyim. No, not at all. The only reason he was%rgoing towards it, was that he had heard that Jessalyn Valios was in the%rarea.%r%tAnd so, it isn't any surprise that he just saunters up the entry%rramp to the ship, and leans as he arrives, "I heard there was a party%rgoin' on here. Hi, Miss Valios."

"I'd be happy to take a look at it," Orson says with an obliging nod,%rlifting a hand and dragging his sleeve across his damp brow. In a%rmoment, he's propped himself up with a knee to right the crate and%rexamine the parts. He gives a mild shrug to Jessalyn and stands, lugging%rthe container. With a start, Orson squints at the new person coming onto%rthe ship. Before he arrives.

Orson relaxes slightly. But not a lot. "Han Solo," he pronounces%rcarefully, lugging the box to the side and setting it on the workstation%rthey've set up. Of course he recognizes him. "Welcome aboard. Feeling%rbetter?" Last time he saw the Corellian, he was unconscious on Gold%rBeach.

The Jedi gets to her feet, swiping her hands together to brush off the%rdust, and straightens her tunic as she notices the new arrival. That's%rthe last person she expected to be looking for her. "General Solo?" she%rsays, a bit dubiously. "A party? I don't think so. We were just.. having%ra um..." She trails off, casting a helpless glance at Orson, and%rclearing her throat. "Demonstration." Pausing, she realizes her rudeness%rand reaches out to shake Han's hand. "Welcome aboard, though, General."

Drew turns to look at the General and straightens reflexively, only to%rrelax again. She's not in the New Republic anymore. She beams him a%rbrief smile and moves over to where Orson is to help him with the parts%ron the floor. No idea /how/ she might be responsible for it crashing%rdown, but she feels guilty anyway. "Y'look a bit peaked, Orson. You%rokay?" she murmurs.

%tHan continues to lean for a moment or two longer, before a smirk%rcovets his maw. "Hey, there," He offers, "I'm feelin' good. Nothin' a%rgood glass of brandy couldn't cure." He hefts the bottles, lowers them%ragain. "I hope I'm not interrupting anything," He offers, and then that%ris confirmed by Jessalyn. "Good. Demonstrations require a good glass of%rbrandy, of course," He states with all the good-intentions as... well.%rHan. So, he proceeds to un-lean, and stroll in further. "How you doin',%rJessalyn? Leia said to say hi," Comes his brusque voice.%r%tAnd then, a few thumpings come into the ship, heavy footsteps,%rfollowed by loud animalesque growls from a similarly un-animalesque%rbeing. Chewbacca, but of course. And he waves heartilly towards%rJessalyn, giving her a greeting rumble.%r%tHan takes Jessalyn's hand in his, and shakes hers briskly, with a%rgrin, "Thanks." That, until he is shoved roughly aside as Chewie moves%rin to lift Jessalyn off her feet in a greeting hug. Well. If you can%rcall being tugged from your feet, and into the arms of a large furred%rbeast.%r%tRather like being thrown into a clothes-dryer in the BRISK setting,%rwith a bunch of cotton-balls. Only softer.

Jessalyn has had a few encounters with Han Solo in the past, but none%rlike this one. She bobs her head as he takes her hand and shakes it,%rgreen eyes blinking widely. "I'm doing fine. Wonderful. Actually," she%rsays, smiling at last -- and that smile only widens as her old friend%rChewbacca comes barelling up the ramp. "Chewie!" is all she has time to%rsqueak before she's enveloped in fur, not to be seen again until the%rWookiee decides to let her go.

Orson gives Han a reluctant nod, shifting his weight and appearing%rentirely uncomfortable with the way the man has become the featured%rattraction. "Good," he says neutrally, but doubts the Corellian hears.

"I'm okay, Drew." The mechanic looks her long-legged shopping self over%rwith pursed lips. "We'll talk later," he promises. Orson stiffens once%rmore as Chewbacca enters, seeming overwhelmed now. At least the%rcomputers were off. Karrde would have some sort of internal breakdown if%rhe knew that one of his ships had just been taken over by this pair.%r"Um, hello."

Drew hovers over the mess and the crate, and since he's short enough,%rOrson too. She readjusts the bag on her shoulder and watches the two%rnewcomers with interest, now that Orson has dismissed her question.%rHe'll hear about that later. She's never really seen the two up close.%rNot without people around like this. Interestink.

%tChewbacca gives a rambling verse to Jessalyn along the lines of%r'good to see you! Why didn't you get in touch! I've missed you after all%rthis time! You wouldn't believe how much trouble Han has gotten me into.%rI could have used you!' And then lets her down in a mostly-gently form,%rand moving a furred paw to fuzzle her red fluff of hair. Well, it would%rprobably turn to fluff now.%r%t"Ah, yes. I forgot you two met. He talks about you, still," Han%roffers, grinning boyishly from the sidelines, turning a gaze towards%rOrson as the pair of furred beings catch up. He waggles his brows%rslightly, observing the man's stance or something like that. Perhaps it%rwould be best for him to drop off a bottle and run.%r%tNo. He was Corellian. "I hope I'm not interrupting, really, I do.%rIf you want me to leave, just holler," He offers casually. He knew when%rto run away and get drunk with Chewie by himself.

Using both hands to smooth her now very mussed and frizzy hair back into%rsome small semblance of order, Jessalyn chatters for a moment with%rChewbacca, at least briefly explaining that she's been, you know, in%rstasis for the past two years or so. Then she smiles a little weakly at%rthe Corellian, moving to stand closer to Drew and Orson at the table,%rher hand resting a little nervously on Orson's shoulder. "General Solo,%rhave you met my friends? This is Drew, one of the crew members on this%rship. And this is my..." All at once none of the explanations for just%rwhat Orson is seem appropriate considering their company, and she%rswallows. "Um. The captain, Orson Tighe. Really, it's their ship, so%rit's up to them...."

"No, that's fine," Orson replies with a casual wave of his hand. "Make%ryourself at home. Here." To clarify all that, he sweeps his hand around%rin a circle, as if to imply 'don't leave this particular room.' Damage%rcontrol.

Eerily, the broad-shouldered mechanic swivels his gaze to Drew. "It's%rokay," he assuages, giving Drew a smooth smile. "I'll explain later." He%rreaches out as if to clap her on the shoulder with a cupped palm, but%rslows his strike and gives her a little awkward pat on the arm instead.%rThe man folds his hand back toward his body and smiles at the group.
"In fact, I need to take care of an errand." With that, he stands%rstraight and delicately removes himself from Jessalyn's grip, giving a%rlittle wave. "Be back in a moment."
Orson sends through the Force...
"It's okay," Orson considers. He's been ready for this, at least this%rpart of it.

Well. Yay, Drew thinks. Another moment where she's left in the dark. She%rblinks back at Orson and watches him leave, then turns to the General.%r"General Solo," she says lightly in greeting, and extends her hand.
Interrupted Lesson
